title: Malty Quenched Dutch Popcorn Puffed Treat
description: A malty, creamy Dutch dish featuring toffee popcorn, perfect for midnight cravings. A flexitarian-friendly treat that's both sweet and savory.
instructions: In a medium saucepan, combine the butter, golden syrup, water, and brown sugar. Heat over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until the sugar dissolves.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer for 5 minutes.
In a separate saucepan, heat the corn syrup over medium heat until it reaches 300°F (150°C) on a candy thermometer.
Remove the corn syrup from the heat and carefully pour it into the saucepan with the sugar mixture. Stir until well combined.
Increase the oven temperature to 425°F (220°C). In a large bowl, drizzle the vegetable oil over the popcorn kernels and toss to coat.
Spread the popcorn mixture onto a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake the popcorn for 10-12 minutes, stirring every 2-3 minutes, until it is toasted and fragrant.
Melt the toffee bits in a microwave-safe bowl in 30-second increments, stirring between each interval, until smooth.
In a large bowl, combine the popcorn, melted toffee, and vanilla extract. Toss until the popcorn is evenly coated.
Pour the malty mixture over the popcorn and toss until well combined.
Spread the mixture onto the prepared baking sheet and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes or until set.
Break the mixture into clusters and serve chilled.
